preamble: The elision of the back vowel [o] is a common sound change, typically occurring as syncope (medial loss) or apocope (final loss). While mid-vowels are more stable than high vowels, they systematically disappear in languages with strong stress-timed rhythms as syllables are compressed.

examples.note: Syncope: loss of the unstressed medial vowel.
Apocope: historical loss of the final vowel after reduction to schwa.
Apocope of final vowels in prepositions before another vowel.
